The 1) Petition for order compelling the Trustee to report and account for surcharge against Trustee for breach of fiduciary duties according to proof, 2) Verified First Amended First and Second Account for the Light Family Living Trust, etc., and 3) Trustee's 17200 Petition to compel Beneficiary Richard Jones to Remit Funds to the Trustee are continued to December 10, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Petition For Order Compelling The Trustee To Report And Account And For Surcharge Against Trustee Fo
Off Calendar Jun-25-2019 Continued To Sep-24-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Cross for and with Richard Jones.
David Simon for Howard Strassner (Trustee).

The Court asks where things stand with regard to compliance with the March 14th order and mediation. Mr. Cross stated that he just substituted in on May 14th as he was off for vacation, that he was unable to do any meeting and conferring, that at the last report there was no compliance with the order, and that he would like to do mediation, but he needs information. Mr. Simon stated that his position is that they have complied with the Court order, that he is hoping to file a response to the objections within the next month, that he will be filing a new 17200 petition for remittance, and requested a referral to mediation and a trial date. Mr. Cross stated that the 2017 statements for the Schwab account and for another account that the trustee used for trust expenses were not produced. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than September 10, 2019. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by September 17, 2019. The Petition for Order Compelling the Trustee to Report and Account, etc., the Verified First and Final Account for Light Family Living Trust and the Trustee's 17200 Petition to Compel Beneficiary Richard Jones to Remit Funds to the Trustee are continued to and the Parties are to return on September 24, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. If there are any new petitions or amendments, the Court expects that they have been filed by then and that the parties cooperate with exchanging information. The parties are to file a joint status statement by September 17th stating where things are, what a trial will look like and what the issues are. The Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel is moot and off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Order Compelling The Trustee To Report And Account And For Surcharge Against Trustee Fo
Off Calendar Dec-05-2018 Continued To Feb-27-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Diana Redding for and with Richard Jones (Petitioner).
David Simon for Howard Strassner (Successor Trustee).

The Parties reported that they are not in agreement as to the language in the proposed order after hearing with respect to the August 29th hearing. The Court noted that the order was not submitted to the Court, that there is no motion before it and spoke of the procedures in place to deal with the issue of when parties can not agree as to the form of order. At the suggestion of the Court, the Parties took a recess to go over the proposed order to try and see if they could come to an agreement as to the form of order. The matter was recalled. Ms. Redding reported that they had not gotten as far as she would have liked, that it did reveal what some of the more pressing issues are, that they agreed to strike out one of the paragraphs and that Mr. Simon does not like the way the order is worded because it looks like an order after a motion to compel. The Court stated that it is prepared to order information, but it is not prepared to order what would be from a motion to compel. Mr. Simon stated that he has provided everything he intends to provide. The Court stated that the Parties need to continue to meet and confer to understand fully as to the Petitioner's position as to what he has requested and as to the Objector's position as to what has been provided, and that the Parties are to finish the meet and confer process. If Ms. Redding still finds there is a roll for this order she can submit it to the Court, Mr. Simon can provide his objections and his proposed form of order, and the Court will determine if it is appropriate to issue an order. The parties agreed on having an accounting through mid January. Mr. Simon stated that he is willing to mediate. Ms. Redding stated that her Client is not willing to do mediation without having information. The Petition for Order Compelling the Trustee to Report and Account, etc., the Verified First and Final Account for Light Family Living Trust and the Trustee's 17200 Petition to Compel Beneficiary Richard Jones to Remit Funds to the Trustee are continued to February 27, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: F. Ruiz. Not reported.

Petition For Order Compelling The Trustee To Report And Account And For Surcharge Against Trustee Fo
Off Calendar Aug-29-2018 Continued To Dec-05-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Simon for Trustee/Respondent.
Diana Redding appeared by telephone for and with Richard Jones (Petitioner/Objector present in Court).

The Court noted that there are two new petitions with hearing dates of September 11th and September 25th, and that there are still issues in the accounting petition that have not been addressed. Mr. Simon stated that they have amended the federal tax returns for 2015 and 2016, that he will be sending them to opposing Counsel today, that they are working on 2017 and that they have come to an agreement on a due by date for compliance regarding discovery. Ms. Redding stated that they have been in communication, that the proposed order has not been returned to her despite the issue as to monetary sanctions, and requested that the Court sign the order and order sanctions. The Court stated that it is not prepared to make an advanced ruling regarding sanctions. The Court will look at the order and assign a compliance date in late November. The Petition for Order Compelling the Trustee to Report and Account, etc. and the First and Final Account, etc. are continued to December 5, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition to Compel Beneficiary to Remit Funds, etc. on calendar for September 11, 2018 is off calendar at Counsel's request. The petition on calendar for September 25, 2018 is continued to December 5, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Gomez. Not reported.

Motion To Compel Petitioner For Reimbursement To The Trust For Expenses Related To Trust Administration And Proposed Order
Off Calendar Mar-07-2018 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Compelling The Trustee To Report And Account And For Surcharge Against Trustee Fo
Off Calendar Jan-17-2018 Continued To Apr-11-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Diana Redding for and with Richard Jones (Beneficiary/Petitioner).
David Simon for and with Howard Strassner (Trustee).

The Court noted that there are a number of issues pointed out by the Examiner that have not been addressed, that a petition for approval of the accounting has been filed and questions whether or not there are any issues remaining on the petition to compel the Trustee to report and account. Mr. Simon stated that the Trustee's position is to resolve this amicably and within the family. Mr. Simon also suggested mediation. Mr. Redding stated that if the procedural issues and the objections are addressed, her Client will be willing to sit down and resolve the matter, but only if all information is disclosed. Ms. Redding also stated that there is missing information that has been requested and asked for a compliance date for exchange of information. The Court noted that the matter does not qualify for Pro Bono Mediation and that the parties will have to do private mediation if they choose. The Petition for Order Compelling the Trustee to Report and Account, etc. and the First and Final Account are continued to April 11, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The parties are to meet and confer to discuss a mechanism for exchanging information. The Court anticipates that the parties either reach an agreement or file a status report no later than April 4, 2018 as to whether or not there are any remaining issues.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Counsel reported that the Trustee provided a confidential accounting on February 15, 2017 and that they agreed to the following: The Trustee will provide a verification and back up documents by March 24, 2017; The Beneficiary will have until April 24, 2017 to inform the Trustee of any objections to the accounting; If the Trustee is not informed of any objections by then, objections will be waived; If there are any objections, the Trustee will file the accounting.
